metadata.source: Cisco Design Guides, Juniper Technical Documentation, Arista EOS Documentation

best_practices: Use loopback interfaces for BGP peering (more stable than physical interfaces)
Configure MD5 authentication for all eBGP peers
Use route policies to filter what is advertised and received
Implement prefix-list based filtering (more efficient than ACL-based)
Use BGP communities for traffic engineering and policy
Configure maximum-prefix limit to protect against route table overflow
Use peer groups for identical neighbor configuration

platform_limits.catalyst_9300: 256K routes default, scalable to 1M with hardware option
platform_limits.isr4000: Full BGP table with adequate RAM

best_practices: Use area design to limit LSA flooding scope
Summarize routes at ABRs to reduce LSA count
Configure authentication for OSPF neighbor relationships
Use passive-interface for non-routing interfaces
Set reference bandwidth for accurate cost calculation: auto-cost reference-bandwidth 100000
Use point-to-point network type on point-to-point links (no DR/BDR election)
BFD for sub-second failure detection

area_types.backbone: Area 0 - all other areas must connect through backbone
area_types.stub: No Type 5 (external) LSAs, uses default route
area_types.totally_stubby: Only default route, no Type 3 summary LSAs (Cisco proprietary)
area_types.nssa: No Type 5 LSAs but allows redistribution via Type 7
area_types.totally_nssa: Only default route, allows redistribution (Cisco proprietary)

best_practices: Use named mode EIGRP for better feature support
Configure authentication with key chains
Set bandwidth on serial interfaces correctly (affects EIGRP metric)
Use summarization at major network boundaries
Stub routers for hub-and-spoke (reduces query scope)

stp.recommended_mode: Rapid PVST+ (default on Catalyst) or MSTP for larger environments
best_practices: Configure root bridge explicitly (do not rely on default priority)
Enable PortFast on all access ports: spanning-tree portfast default
Enable BPDU Guard on all access ports: spanning-tree bpduguard enable
Enable Root Guard on distribution-to-access uplinks
Enable Loop Guard on redundant links
Enable BPDU Filter only if PortFast used without BPDU Guard (careful)

vlan_design.naming_convention: Descriptive names: DATA, VOICE, MGMT, NATIVE, PRINTERS
vlan_design.native_vlan: Change native VLAN from default (VLAN 1): switchport trunk native vlan 999
vlan_design.vlan_1: Never use VLAN 1 for user traffic - management and security risk
vlan_design.voice_vlan: Separate VLAN for IP phones: switchport voice vlan {id}

vss.description: Virtual Switching System - two Catalyst switches operate as single logical switch

versions.v2: Supports groups 0-4095, uses multicast 224.0.0.102, IPv6 support

asr1000.use_case: WAN edge, internet edge, service provider

models.ASR1001-X: Up to 20 Gbps, fixed form factor
models.ASR1002-HX: Up to 100 Gbps, fixed high performance

bgp.policy_framework: Juniper uses policy-statement with from/then structure for routing policies
bgp.commit_model: Configuration changes staged in candidate config, then committed atomically

best_practices: Set reference-bandwidth to 100g for accurate cost on 10G+ interfaces
Use interface-type p2p on point-to-point links
BFD for fast failure detection
Passive interface equivalent: passive in interface config

junos_specifics.configuration_model: Hierarchical configuration, candidate config, atomic commits
junos_specifics.rollback: junos supports automatic rollback: rollback 1 (rollback to previous config)

commits.confirmed: commit confirmed 5 (auto-rollback in 5 minutes if not confirmed)

operational_commands.show_bgp: show bgp summary / show bgp neighbor <ip>
operational_commands.show_ospf: show ospf neighbor / show ospf interface
operational_commands.show_interfaces: show interfaces terse / show interfaces detail

bgp.note: Arista EOS uses Cisco-like CLI (VEOS), making migration easier from Cisco

7800r3.use_case: Large data center spine, cloud provider

cloudvision.description: Network-wide streaming telemetry and configuration management

unique_features.extensible_os: EOS can run Linux processes in VRF (container-based extensions)
unique_features.streaming_telemetry: Native OpenConfig/gNMI support from day one

zone_design.template:
security zone design:
  zones: [trust, untrust, dmz, mgmt]
  policies: zone_to_zone with application-based rules
  best_practice: deny all inter-zone by default, whitelist required

ha_modes.active_active: Both process traffic, different virtual addresses
ha_modes.active_standby: One processes, other standby with connection mirroring
